title: Black Bean Chicken

overview.description: Tender chicken stir-fried with peppers and onions in a rich savoury black bean sauce. A takeaway favourite that's surprisingly quick to make at home and packed with authentic flavour.

ingredients: 600g chicken thighs, sliced into bite-sized pieces
2 tbsp fermented black beans (douchi), rinsed and roughly chopped
method: Mix the cornflour with the water to make a smooth slurry and set aside.
Heat a wok or large frying pan over a high heat until very hot.
Add the vegetable oil and stir-fry the chicken for 5–6 minutes until lightly browned. Remove and set aside.
Add the onion and peppers and stir-fry for about 3 minutes until beginning to soften but still retaining some bite.
Add the garlic, ginger and chopped fermented black beans and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the chicken to the pan.
Add the light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar and chicken stock. Stir well and bring to a simmer.
Stir in the cornflour slurry and cook for 1–2 minutes until the sauce has thickened and coats the chicken.
Scatter with the spring onions and serve immediately.

learning.did-you-know: Fermented black beans are soybeans, not black beans. Their intense savoury flavour has been used in Cantonese cooking for centuries.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to stir-fry over high heat, how fermented black beans differ from bottled sauces, and how a simple cornflour slurry creates a glossy restaurant-style sauce.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the anchor recipes for Chicken Around the World. It brings authentic Cantonese flavours into the planner while remaining quick enough for a busy weekday evening.
chapman-tips: Prepare every ingredient before you start cooking—the stir-fry moves very quickly.
Chicken thighs remain juicier than breast in a hot wok.
Don't overcook the peppers; a little crunch gives the dish its character.
notes.batch-cooking: Best cooked fresh, although leftovers freeze well for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently and serve with freshly cooked rice.
